danger_signals.visual_form: Bullets fired by tanks, gunboats, aircraft, and artillery
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion or interception; collision causes damage/death

danger_signals.visual_form: Ground and air enemies (tanks, gunboats, aircraft) positioned across terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Concentrated danger zones; multiple simultaneous threats requiring tactical positioning

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy bullets hitting carried bombs automatically detonate them
danger_signals.meaning: Double-edged mechanic—protective but risky if positioned near enemies

danger_signals.visual_form: Player death resets to last checkpoint after losing a life
danger_signals.meaning: Progress loss; danger of cascading failures if multiple lives lost in sequence

safety_signals.visual_form: Green-colored cross-shaped bunkers spawn 'B' icon pickup
safety_signals.meaning: Short-range defensive/offensive capability; area-denial tool for bullet clearing

safety_signals.visual_form: Red or gray-colored bunker pickups spawn smaller helicopters to join formation
safety_signals.meaning: Additional firepower; enhanced survivability through formation support

safety_signals.visual_form: Accumulated points and destruction of ten diamond-shaped blocks award extra lives
safety_signals.meaning: Extended survival capacity; buffer against attrition

safety_signals.visual_form: Level completion landmark where player lands after each stage
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone transition; bonus points awarded for unused ordnance (bombs, wingmen)

safety_signals.visual_form: Helicopter movement is deliberately slower than alternative aircraft
safety_signals.meaning: Improved bullet visibility and strategic route planning; reduced stress for beginners

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Starts missions from unspecified origin; returns to helipad after each stage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Militaristic hardware: tanks, gunboats, artillery, aircraft
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types (air vs. ground) with distinct silhouettes and attack patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scattered across diverse terrains: buildings, level crossings, fuel depots, tents, mine carts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sparse enemy density; slower projectile patterns; bunkers with power-up items visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies present predictable attack patterns; beginners can identify bullet trajectories more easily

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased enemy formations; coordinated air and ground threats; bunker items may be harder to access safely
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy concentration forces player into tighter tactical routes; bomb detonation becomes critical defense

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Helipad landmark appears; arena clears of active threats
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Safe transition zone; bonus scoring phase for ordnance retention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game returns to stage 2 with higher difficulty visual density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy spawn rates and projectile speed increase; terrain remains consistent but threat escalates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player death animation; checkpoint reset visual cue
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission resets to last checkpoint; tactical regrouping required; stress/tension conveyed through restart

environmental_storytelling.example: Four distinct stages with different environments (implied by level progression structure) communicate military campaign across varied operational areas
Targetable buildings, fuel depots, tents, mine carts, and level crossings suggest supply line disruption mission; destroying civilian/logistical infrastructure indicates war scenario
Tanks, gunboats, artillery scattered across terrain suggest entrenched defensive positions; air threats indicate air superiority contest
Return to helipad after each stage suggests forward operating base/staging area; implies ongoing campaign with resupply points

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Vertical scroll following with responsive lateral and vertical maneuvering; slower speed allows deliberate positioning
Defensive posture; area-denial capability; risk of collision-triggered detonation increases threat awareness
Formation geometry changes (left/right variants with lateral or forward firing); additional firepower reduces individual risk exposure
Ground-based stationary or slow-moving threat; fires upward at helicopter; targetable from distance
Air-to-air threat; matches vertical scroll speed; requires missile interception or evasion
Water-based threat; fires projectiles; indicates coastal/river operations
Destructible objective; color state determines reward type (bomb vs. wingmen); visual toggle conveys switching reward availability
Slower projectile speed (design feature for visibility); destroyable by bombs; collision hazard with helicopter or carried ordnance

notes: Tiger-Heli employs military hardware iconography and combat mission framing (supply destruction, entrenched positions) without explicit faction narrative. World-state communication relies on enemy density escalation, environmental variety (suggesting strategic locations), and checkpoint/helipad structures implying campaign progression. Visual design prioritizes clarity for beginners through slower craft speed and bullet visibility—danger is communicated through projectile patterns rather than visual grotesquerie or color saturation shifts. Safety signals operate mechanically (bunker items, wingmen) rather than narratively. The game's minimalist visual narrative—helicopter vs. generic enemy forces across diverse military infrastructure—positions it as abstract tactical conflict rather than specific geopolitical scenario.
